body.title: Wire EvaluatorDevice into sprint-ticket step 8.5 post-sprint grader
body.result: sprint_grader.py built: EvaluatorDevice-backed post-sprint grader with pass/partial/fail verdict; stamp-verdict added to cc_queue.py; SKILL.md step 8.5 updated; stale cc_worker_listener test fixed
body.intention: I intend that post-sprint evaluation uses EvaluatorDevice instead of an ad-hoc Haiku subagent, so verdicts are stamped to ticket metadata and queryable via eval_history.
body.description:
The /sprint-ticket skill step 8.5 spawns an ad-hoc Haiku subagent to grade the diff against the test plan. This verdict is ephemeral — it exists only in the conversation and is not stored anywhere. EvaluatorDevice already provides evaluate() with persistent history via eval_history().

Replace the ad-hoc subagent in step 8.5 with: EvaluatorDevice.evaluate(output=git_diff, rubric_id=post-sprint-verdict, agent_id=ticket_id). Create the post-sprint-verdict rubric in palace: criteria are test plan coverage, scope boundary adherence, completion criteria met.

Stamp the verdict into ticket metadata so cc_queue.py show surfaces it. Close T-judge-agent (design status, superseded).

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md (step 8.5 rewrite); devices/evaluator/ (rubric creation at setup time); lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (stamp verdict to ticket metadata)
**Design rules:** no-autonomous-action (verdict is advisory, never closes ticket automatically); no SQLite
**Scope boundary:** IN: step 8.5 uses EvaluatorDevice; post-sprint rubric created; verdict stamped to ticket. OUT: verdict affecting sprint flow; automatic ticket closure; changing when step 8.5 fires.
**Completion criteria:**
  1. After a sprint, cc_queue.py show <id> includes a verdict field with pass/fail/partial
  2. EvaluatorDevice.eval_history(agent_id=ticket_id) returns the verdict
  3. T-judge-agent closed as superseded
  4. Existing sprint-ticket tests pass
